title: ContentPack
description: A content pack (#241): the top-level unit of an official game's content repository. Bundles authorized IE sites, a customFileSystem fragment, a scenario, per-culture string tables, and an asset manifest. Every field is optional.

id.description: Stable id — namespaces the asset cache and feeds the snapshot fingerprint.

assets.description: Logical asset key → concrete source, resolved once when the host mounts the pack. Values should be inline or { url } sources, not { asset } (which would be an indirection loop).

sites.description: IE authorized-site registry. Keys are URLs (normalized at load time, rules per #149); an authorized page always wins over the search corpus / Wayback fallback.

files.description: customFileSystem fragment. File nodes may carry `contentRef` in place of inline `content`.

recycleBin.description: Authored Recycle Bin records with stable paths and deletion times.

recentDocuments.description: Historical MRU entries merged with runtime file opens.

scenario.description: The scenario rulebook (#84/#207). See docs/SCENARIOS.md for its schema.

strings.description: Per-culture string tables: culture id → key → text (#207 copy-extraction carrier).

contentRef.title: ContentRef
contentRef.description: A reference to a piece of content. Three sources, one shape.

oneOf.description: A host asset URL resolved at load time (build import / public path / CDN).
A logical key resolved through the pack's `assets` manifest — the portable reference.

html.description: The page body to render (inline HTML, a host url, or an asset key).

title.description: Browser/tab title; falls back to the site's URL key when omitted.

favicon.description: Optional favicon (icon id, data URI, or a ContentRef).
